title: Magically Crispy Venezuelan Buckwheat Empanadas
description: A crispy and savory Venezuelan dish featuring buckwheat and katsuramuki-cut ingredients, perfect for a date night, with a bitter taste and low glycemic index, this recipe is a unique twist on traditional empanadas.

ingredients.name: buckwheat flour
ingredients.notes: make sure to use gluten-free buckwheat flour

instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, mix together the buckwheat flour and salt.
Gradually add the water to the bowl and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Flatten each ball into a disk shape using a rolling pin.
Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat and cook the onion and garlic until softened.
Add the ground beef to the skillet and cook until browned, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
Stir in the cumin and paprika and cook for 1 minute.
Place a tablespoon of the beef mixture onto the center of each dough disk and fold the dough in half to form a half-moon shape.
Brush the tops of the empanadas with a little bit of water and bake for 20 minutes or until crispy and golden brown.
Cook ground beef to an internal temperature of 71°C / 160°F (no longer pink in the middle) before serving.
